revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]][[ja:藤本観音山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun | native_name =藤本観音山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Fujimotokannonyamakofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.300778|N|139.43675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ashikaga|qid=Q374477|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun'''|藤本観音山古墳 |}} is a {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|qid=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2006.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192712|title=藤本観音山古墳|trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=September 1, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun is located in the Fujimoto neighborhood of Ashikaga City surrounded by rice fields, in the northern end of the {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|qid=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}} near the border with {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is located on a plateau on the right bank of the Yaba River. The surrounding area has numerous smaller ''kofun'' and was densely settled in the ancient period.
The tumulus has a total length of 117.8 meters, and consists of a front part with two tiers and a height of 5.4 meters, and a back part with three tiers and a height of 11.8 meters. In the rear part, ''[[fukiishi]]'' covered the third tier, and although no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found, a clay pot with a double rim was placed on top of the mound. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, and including the dimensions of the moat, the total length exceeds 210 meters. This is the fifth largest ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' in Japan, and the third largest in eastern Japan. It dates from the middleof the 4th century, based on a {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|qid=Q180044|lt=Pumice|1=Pumice}} layer from a 4th century eruption of {{ill|Mount Asama|en|Mount Asama|qid=Q242730|lt=Mount Asama|1=Mount Asama}} which was found in the topsoil beneath the tumulus.
The tumulus has not been excavated and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] appears to be intact, as no trace of digging has been detected. The first excavation survey was carried out in 1984, and further surveys were conducted for seven times by 2003. On the south side of tumulus, the remains of 21 [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and three storage pits dating from the same time as the ''kofun'' have been discovered. It is believed that this was a settlement either related to the construction of the tumulus, or connected to rituals associated with the ''kofun'' after its cgoodbye $1.
The ''kofun'' is located approximately a 30 minute walk from {{ill|Fukui Station (Tochigi)|en|Fukui Station (Tochigi)|qid=Q5507654|lt=Fukui Station|1=Fukui Station (Tochigi)}} on the {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|qid=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line|1=Tobu Isesaki Line}}.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
